description: Architectural props and structural elements placed in BiomeB. All structures are geological/natural formations consistent with Cretaceous era — no human-built structures. These are ancient stone formations shaped by erosion, volcanic activity, and time.

spawned_actors.label: Arch_StoneRuinPillar_01
spawned_actors.narrative: Tallest pillar — visible from distance, acts as landmark

spawned_actors.label: Arch_StoneRuinPillar_03
spawned_actors.narrative: Third pillar — completes the triangular formation

spawned_actors.label: Arch_StoneRuinWall_01
spawned_actors.narrative: Fallen stone slab — creates natural windbreak and shelter opportunity for player

pending_assets.prompt: Ancient weathered stone slab wall section, Cretaceous era, moss-covered limestone, crumbling edges, game-ready low-poly, PBR textures, Unreal Engine 5
Natural cave entrance arch formed from limestone rock, Cretaceous era, stalactites visible, moss and ferns growing, game-ready low-poly prop, PBR textures, Unreal Engine 5

design_notes.philosophy: Every structure in BiomeB is a geological document. The triangular pillar formation creates a natural arena — defensible position for the player, hunting ground for predators. The stone slab provides first-tier shelter: blocks wind, hides from line-of-sight.
design_notes.gameplay_function: Pillars = navigation landmarks visible at 500m. Wall slab = tier-1 shelter (no crafting required). Cave arch (pending) = tier-2 shelter with temperature regulation.
design_notes.anti_spiritual_check: PASS — all structures are natural geological formations. No sacred sites, no ritual markers, no spiritual significance. Pure survival utility.
design_notes.next_agent_handoff: Agent #08 Lighting should add point lights or emissive materials near pillar bases to make them visible at night. Shadow casting from pillars creates tactical cover indicators.
